channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19915] keep client configurations on MDTs/OSTs in sync with MGS0
item.description:
Attach local storage per config (aka cld) to store llog config files, so that
these llogs are kept in sync with MGS0 on all MDTs/OSTs.
item.summary: keep client configurations on MDTs/OSTs in sync with MGS0

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment: The MGS replicas have sptlrpc/params/<fsname>-client/IR-tables in sync with MGS0, and AFAICS the nodemap is kept in sync by itself. And each target has the config for itself, but not configs for other targets.
Lai, this version of the patches, does it support all of the MGS functionality (client+server config/params llogs, nodemap configs, IR table), or only the client config llogs?

In summary, replicating config files by MGC can only achieve eventual consistency, but it's enough for client mount, while for services like FLDB and Quota, strong consistency is needed, so RAFT is still needed.

So even as we implement MGS upon RAFT, the MGS0 is still not fault-tolerant, the final result is the same as this implementation.

That would only affect the initial target registration? It should be enough that the targets specify multiple MGS NIDs on startup, so that at least one of them is running when they try to connect. One possibility to simplify this is to use DNS or Rendezvous protocol to handle MGS hostname lookup to get the MGS0 NID so that it could be running on any server node (LU-19412). However, that is a low priority compared to having a redundant MGS to handle the client configuration, since client mounting is happening all the time.

One possible way to implement fault tolerant MGS0 is as below:

  1. start MGS0 and targets like before
  2. when RAFT cluster is up, and leader elected, MGS0 notify all targets to switch to RAFT leader as MGS

But even with this, it's better to sync the configuration files and IR data via MGC like this patch set because it's more lightweight. And this involves a few changes:

  • MGS0 needs to get the nids of RAFT leader
  • MGC on targets need to be stopped and restart with RAFT leader nids as connection nids, and targets need to register again to ensure target configuration files are created
  • the config change commands like 'lctl set_param -p' that run on mgs host need to be forwarded to RAFT leader

IMHO FLDB and Quota still need RAFT to be fault tolerant, since it's the standard way to implement replicated services. As for MGS, it's different from other services: the MGS is started before RAFT cluster is setup, and MDTs/OSTs mkfs with the MGS nid set, and they need the configuration from MGS to fully setup (possibly not if they have backup in local storage). So even as we implement MGS upon RAFT, the MGS0 is still not fault-tolerant, the final result is the same as this implementation.

One of the future goals for FTM is that common infrastructure could be used to store databases for MDT0000 to make the filesystem more robust in case of failure and to allow MDT0000 removal from the filesystem for hardware replacement (LU-18823). This covers information like quota limits (QMT), FLDB, flock, etc. While those databases also do not change rapidly, they cannot be "offline" for a long time if a server has a hardware problem. Do you have a proposal to handle replicating these databases without RAFT?

It would be possible to do this by e.g. expanding the FLDB replication to copy the full table to all nodes (it is relatively small, and most targets already have a good fraction of the table) and developing a mechanism for transfer of the master service, but it would need similar efforts for all of this functionality.

Yes, this implementation is much lightweight, and it can also support standalone MGS0. BTW the MGS replica will be started on OST nodes as well.

The backup MGS won't take over MGS0 as primary, but wait for MGS0 to come back. If MGS0 node can not get back, the administrator has two options:

  1. if MGS0 is standalone, recreate MGS0 and copy configuration files from other nodes to this new MGS0
  2. specify one server node as MSG0, and restart all server nodes

Lai, is this totally replacing the previous FTM implementation with RAFT?

It seems like this will replicate the logs onto the other MDTs, and run an MGS service on the other MDS nodes, but what happens if the primary MGS fails? Will one of the backup MGS nodes take over as primary, or can nothing change in the configuration if the primary MGS is offline?

"Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/64201
Subject: LU-19915 mgc: attach local storage per cld
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: b1d2f5046ef6f101da9341229790ef729b7a7a69
